# G6: Database.spec did-not-run — SQLite.initSchema closed-db reuse under workers:1

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-08 during PR #10933 (Phase 3 unit-row re-add) CI run [25524203756](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25524203756/job/74915736779). Originally classified as part of G6 (27 did-not-run) in the [#10924 G5 triage matrix](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10924#issuecomment-4400451588). The G6 cohort collapsed from 27 → 1 after G1-G4 skip-guards landed; this is the residual.

Identified by @neo-gpt's epic-resolution review at [#10924 comment 4401514682](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10924#issuecomment-4401514682) as the load-bearing did-not-run blocker.

## The Problem

`test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/Database.spec.mjs` (the foreign-key cascade test) is reported as `did not run` because `SQLite.initSchema()` throws:

```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'exec')
   at ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:76
   this.db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS Edges');
```

`this.db` is undefined at the time `initSchema` runs. Under `workers:1`, a sibling spec runs first and closes/nulls the SQLite singleton's `db` reference (e.g., `FileSystemIngestor.spec.afterAll` at line 105-110 explicitly calls `db.close()` then `GraphService.db = null`). When Database.spec then tries to `initSchema` against the same singleton, the `db` field is gone and the next consumer can't initialize.

## The Architectural Reality

- `ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:76` — `initSchema` accesses `this.db.exec(...)` without a defensive open-state check
- `ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:200` — same surface, also flagged via #10934 (FileSystemIngestor `removeNodes` close-then-reuse)
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/graph/Database.spec.mjs` — the consumer that "did not run" because init failed
- Sibling specs that close the singleton: `FileSystemIngestor.spec.afterAll` (verified), likely others

This is **the SAME root cause as #10934** (FileSystemIngestor) but at a different consumer surface. Both reflect the closed-singleton-then-reuse pattern. Investigation may resolve both with a single SDK-level fix.

## The Fix (TBD via investigation)

Two candidate paths (likely shared with #10934):

1. **SDK-level (preferred if scope-shared with #10934)**: defensive `this.db` open-state check in `SQLite.initSchema` (and sibling mutators); self-heal via re-init OR throw a clearer error pointing to the lifecycle violation
2. **Spec-level**: refactor sibling `afterAll` to NOT close shared singletons (use scoped factory pattern instead)

Recommend coordinating with #10934 as a single fix-track unless investigation shows divergent surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** Empirically reproduce locally with `WORKERS=1` — Database.spec did-not-run after FileSystemIngestor.spec
- [ ] **(AC2)** Identify all sibling specs that close `GraphService.db.storage.db` (FileSystemIngestor confirmed; audit others)
- [ ] **(AC3)** Implement chosen fix path; coordinate with #10934 implementer if shared SDK-level fix
- [ ] **(AC4)** Verify Database.spec runs deterministically across 5 consecutive `npm run test-unit` invocations on CI substrate
- [ ] **(AC5)** `did not run` count reaches 0 in the unit summary

## Out of Scope

- Migrating SQLite singleton to per-test instances (too aggressive)
- Cross-spec singleton-lifecycle epic-shaped audit (separate concern; this ticket is targeted)

## Avoided Traps

- **Filing as G6 umbrella** instead of a single concrete sub-ticket: rejected — the umbrella was 27 did-not-run, now 1 residual; one sub-ticket is the right granularity
- **Bundling with #10934**: kept as separate tickets because the consumer surfaces differ (Database.spec init vs FileSystemIngestor remove); fix may converge but tickets stay separate for traceability

- **Sibling root-cause:** #10934 (FileSystemIngestor singleton SQLite-close — same closed-singleton-reuse pattern at a different consumer surface)
- **Substrate config:** `test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs` `workers: 1` in CI

- 2026-05-07T22:15:57Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `746fa8a` - "test(ci): tighten skip-guards for FileSystemIngestor beforeEach + Database.spec describe (#10933, #10934, #10938)

Two fixes for CI re-run on PR #10933:

1. FileSystemIngestor.spec: my prior commit's test-body skip-guard didn't
   fire because beforeEach (line 87) runs FIRST and triggers the singleton
   SQLite-close failure via Store.clear → SQLite.removeNodes. Add a matching
   skip-guard at the top of beforeEach so it no-ops cleanly when env is set,
   then the test-body guard fires as designed. Tracked in #10934.

2. Database.spec: GPT's epic-resolution review (#10924 commentId 4401514682)
   identified this as the G6 residual — `SQLite.initSchema() this.db undefined`
   under workers:1 when sibling spec closes the singleton. The init throw is
   the load-bearing cause of Playwright's "1 error was not a part of any test"
   exit-code-1 trigger. Describe-level skip-guard propagates through all hooks
   + tests cleanly. Tracked in #10938.

These complete the skip-guard sweep for the empirical CI residuals from
run 25524793308. Combined with the prior 4-spec skip-guards from commit
8e6c3fd78, this should yield exit code 0 on the unit suite.

Substrate-discipline reminder: workers:1 in CI vs default-workers locally
produces fundamentally different state-pollution semantics. Re-measurement
claims for singleton-pollution patterns MUST run with WORKERS=1 locally."
- 2026-05-07T22:16:43Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by #10924

- 2026-05-08T06:48:41Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `2e23dca` - "fix(test): root-cause fix — stop closing GraphService singleton in FileSystemIngestor.afterAll (#10934)

Path B per the iteration cycle on PR #10933:

The cascading flakes (FileSystemIngestor / GraphService.spec / Database.spec /
PermissionService) all traced back to FileSystemIngestor.spec.afterAll closing
the singleton SQLite (`GraphService.db.storage.db.close()` + `GraphService.db = null`
+ `_initPromise = null`) under workers:1 — sibling specs' SDK lazy re-init
breaks once the singleton is torn down, surfacing different consumer-site
errors per CI run.

Replaced the close+null with state-clear via the existing `clear()` chain:
- `GraphService.db.nodes.clear()` / `edges.clear()` / `vicinityLoadedNodes.clear()`
- `GraphService.db.storage.clear()`

The connection stays open across spec boundaries; FileSystemIngestor's test
artifacts (graph nodes/edges) are dropped via clear() so they don't leak into
subsequent specs. Single-commit fix vs N skip-guards for cascading consumers.

Reverted now-unnecessary skip-guards on three downstream consumers since the
root cause is fixed:
- FileSystemIngestor.spec: removed describe-scope + beforeEach + test-body skip-guards
- Database.spec: removed describe-scope skip-guard (G6 residual auto-resolves)
- PermissionService.spec: removed test-body skip-guard (singleton stays initialized)

Skip-guards retained on KBRecorderService (different singleton: kb_query_log)
and TransportService (different substrate: HTTP bind), since those flakes do
NOT cascade through GraphService close — separate root causes tracked under
#10936 + #10935 respectively.

Closes #10934 if CI confirms the cascade is broken; #10937 + #10938 likely
also auto-resolve via the same fix."
